subject: Computer Networking
title: Mosaic: The Browser That Changed Everything
description: Learn Mosaic: The Browser That Changed Everything in one minute.

hook.text: The web existed for two years before most people saw it. Mosaic made it visual, clickable, and accessible.

buildup.text: Released in 1993 by NCSA, Mosaic displayed images inline with text. Previous browsers were text-only.

discovery.text: It ran on Windows, Mac, and Unix. Anyone with a PC and a modem could browse the web for the first time.

twist.text: Mosaic's lead developer, Marc Andreessen, left to co-found Netscape — launching the dot-com era.

climax.text: In one year, web traffic jumped from 1% to 20% of internet backbone traffic. Mosaic was the catalyst.

punchline.text: Mosaic showed the world what the web could look like.

quiz.question: What was revolutionary about the Mosaic browser?
options: It displayed images inline with text on multiple platforms
It was the first browser to use HTTPS
